Allsuch scholars shallbe full- time scholars (Ref. 1.7). 4.4 Other Admission Provisions i) Admissio.r to the Ph.D. Prograrnmes shall be held throughout the year subject to thp holdhg ofthe meeting ofDRC and JDRC. ii) The admission procedue shall form a part of the academic celendar, clearly stating the dat6s for adveftisement, issue of application forms, rec€ipt of applioation, meetings ofDRCs datc of deciaration of admission results, last date for pa], eht of fees, commeocement of programme etc. iii) The selectioo of scholars shall be made by the rcspective DRCs after giving due consideration to the qualificqtions and on interviewing the candidates, such that: - The candidates possess the rcquisit€ qualificatiors and experiencr along with competence for the pmposed research. - The research on the proposed subjecr can be succ€ssfully pursued. - The res€arch work can be appropriately undertaken in the concemed Department ofStudies. iv) All the seleations shall be collectively ratified in the Joint Departmeffai Research Committee (JDRC) under the Chairmanship of Dean (Academic). v) The topic given in the application shall be considered as indicative of the area of rcsearch. vi) The scholars shall have the freedom to refine the topic within twelve months of regisuation. vii) In case the research topic of soholar requires multidisciplinary input, at the time of admission, the scholar shall be assigned to the department \yhioh would provide the major input to that topic and the selection of such scholars may be made by DRC. RESIDENCY l0.l In case of full time scholars, every scholar is required to devote his eDtire time to doctoral research during three years after registration during which h€/ she shall be resident. 10.2 Arty scholar living within 50 kfis. ofthe School shall be considered as resident. 10.3 In case of part-time students, the Residency Requirement shall be op€Etive only for the period during which the soholar is undertaking the prescribed course work at the Slhool as prescribed in the rules. Affer satisfactory completion ol the compulsory, optiooal and other recommeoded course work, the scholar, shall be free to work on his / her thesis sn,.where, provided he / she stays in constant touch with his / her Supervisor / Chairperson DRC and attends the DRC meaings regularly. 10.4 In exceptional cases, the pedod of residence for a scholar may be staggered on the recommendation ofthe DRC and approval ofthe Chairperson JDRC. 10.5 A scholar during the period of residerce of otre a.d a half year may be permitted by the Supervisor to go out of Delhi, fiom time to time, as per the requirements of his res€arch work. Out station stay of the scholar exceeding one month may b€ pemritted on the rcooEmendation of the supervisor and with the knowledge alld approval of the Chairperson DRC. II. PROGRESS OT RESEARCE I l.l Every scholar shall submit a progress repofl of his work in the prescribed forms to the Supervisor at the end of every six months. The Supervisor will forward six monthly progress reports with hiv her remarks for the considemtion ofthe DRC. After reviewing the report, the DRC may recommend appropriate aotion to be taken by Cpp and JDRC. Every scholar shall, before the end ofone year after registration, submit a time schedule of his work as plfined for the next one year to DRC and to Cpp. ll.2 If there are thr€e succsssive unsatisfactory rcports (or absents from the me€ting of tfie DRC) on the progress of a scholar as revieved by DRC"/ Cpp or in case of brcach of prescribed rules, the Chairpenon JDRC may recommend cancellation ofthe registration of the soholar on the rccommendation ofthe DRC/ Cpp. 12. PREPARATION OF TIIESIS l2.l The thesis shall be an original work which should signify discovery of new facts or irdioate Irew techniques or nerv inler-relations offacts already koown. The articulation of the contents should be satisfactory, both in rext ard graphic presentation. The respective supervisors wil! ensure that the thesis has b€en put to plagiarism ch€ck and it meets the norms as decided by the JDRC from time ro fime. plagiarism in any case shall not be more than l5 per cent and a certificate to this €ffect will be enclosei along with the final thesiE at the time ofsubmission ofthesis and viva-voce examination. 9 -- 10 of 19 -- '# ixffi:""i,["#;'J:f"::ffi:ifli :J"t""l]::- "" : : ::::-":::"-,:. After these modifications the viva-voce exam ination ofthe scholar may be held; c) :lhe thesis shall be ref€rred to the scholar for revision foireasons in the report, and thercafter the thesis shall be re-examined by ihe same examiners; and d) The thesis shall be rejected for rhe reasons as staled. 14,5 Reminder may be ssnt to the examiners by Cpp after six weeks. If report is not rcceived after eight we€ks, last rerninder may be sent for next two weeks. 14.6 Ifthe report is not rec€ived with in. next two \reeks ofextension pe od, Cpp will bring it to notice ofchairperson JDRC for further necess.ry aotion. 14-7 Chailpe.son JDRC may send to arothcr examiner from the lisl r€commended by Chairpenoo JDRC and approved by Chairman Senate. 15. CONDUCT OF YIVA.VOCE EXAMINATION I5.l Viva-voce examinations will be conducted if a) All the exaoiners mention recommendations as per clause l4,4.aot 14.4.b. b) Ifany examine(s) gives under 14.4.c. Thesis, after iDcorporating the c4mments, will be examined by the sarne examine(s) and iffinal re.commendation by this examiner is 14.4.a or 14.4.b, the viva-voce examinations may be conducted_ c) ln case ofdifferenc€s ofopinion between the examiners, views oftwo examiners out ofthree will prevail. d) A,thesis requiring major revisions may be submitte.d within six months ofthe recoipt of decision of the School. The same shall normally be examined by tlre alaminer recommending major revisions. If the examircr is unable to re-cvaluate the thesiq the Director, io consultation wift the Dean, Cpp and Chairperson DRC shall appini another examiner or other examinerc. The administration of the School shal, assast the Programme Coordinator in terms of accounts and administration related to the programme. 11. The Programme Coordinator shall prepare a Budget for the programme and have the same approved by the Director before conduct of the programme- 12. The Budget of the programme shall comprise of heads of expenditure viz. preparatory travel and stay connected with the programme if required, preparation of course material, honorarium to resource persons for preparing course material, stationery, binding, course kit, group photo, hiring of AV equipment if required, hiring of staff on ad- hoc/termporary basis, hiring of local conveyance, preparation of certificates, food, speaker remuneration/ travel /stay if required, honorarium for Programme Coordinator/ speakers/faculty / support staff ( from SPA or from outside ), momentos, decoration, local as well as outstation travel, misc.. items, etc. depending on the requirements of the programme. Out station travel and stay for field visits as part of the programme are also permitted as per requirements of the programme. The Programme Coordinator shall draw advances and render accounts lor the conduct of the programme. 13. For the work put in by the Programme Coordinator / Faculty member of the School for the conduct of the programme, which is in addition to the normal teaching load of the concerned Faculty, the Programme Coqrdinator/s / Faculty member/s are eligible for receiving remuneration. The remuneration amount needs to be provided for in the budget. The remuneration amount can vary, dependjng on the amount of fund raised and available in the programme. The remuneration shall not be more than THREE months Gross Salary (Basic + GP+DA+HRA) per year. Such remuneration received is not to be treated as Consultancy. 14. The Programme Coordinator shall prepare the Budget and have the same approved by the Director of the School before conducting the programme. 15. All unspent funds in the Budget shall be appropriated to the School account and 30% of the same shall be made available to the concerned Department_ 16. School of Planning and Architecture, New Delhi Guidelines for Gonduct of Research Projects in the School All faculty members of the School are encouraged to take up research projects / studies in various areas related to human settlement development. Faculty members may negotiate with various governments/ministries/ departments / PSUS / funding agencies / para statal agencies / ULBS / NGOS/CBOs / international agencies, educational institutions, universities, industry, etc. for sponsorship to conducl research studies/poects. 1 2 5 3 4 The research work / studies may be conducted by a faculty member of the School or in collaboration with a consortium of institutions. The research work may be conducted either in the School premises or in hired premises depending on the situation and speciflc requirements of the study/project work. All funds received from the sponsors of such research projec'ts to be undertaken by a Faculty member of the School shall be received in the name of SCHOOL OF PLANNING AND ARCHITECTURE, NEW DELHI, by way of a crossed cheque / online and deposited in the account of the School. Any research work shall be conducted by one or more faculty members who shall be called the'Research Project Coordinator'. He/she shall be incharge for all mafters related to the project starting from putting up a note to the Director seeking approval for undertaking the project, negotiating with the sponsors, preparing the budget, arranging the resource persons ( if any) to be associated with the project, managing and conducting the poect, hiring staff, involving students, undertaking surveys, preparing reports, etc. The Research Project Coordinator shall involve students to the extent possible and students can also be remunerated for this purpose. However, this should be done in a manner so that it does not affect the regular student obligations and participation in the academic programme for which he/she is enrolled in the School. The administration of the School shall assist the Research Project Coordinator in terms of accounts and administration related to the research work / study.
